narration.essentials: The processor starts by fetching the first instruction.
narration.detailed: Every cycle begins with a fetch: the processor reads the instruction at the program counter from memory, then decodes it.
narration.expert: Fetch: the PC (0x00000000) indexes instruction memory; the word is latched into the decoder and the PC is prepared to advance by 4.

narration.essentials: t0 becomes the loop counter, set to 10.
narration.detailed: t0 is the loop counter, initialized to 10. The loop will add 10, then 9, and so on down to 1.

narration.essentials: The loop body adds the counter into the running total.
narration.detailed: The heart of the loop: `add a0, a0, t0` adds the current counter value into a0, the running total. On this first pass a0 becomes 10.
narration.expert: The register file reads a0 and t0, the ALU adds them, and the result is written back to a0 in the same cycle.

narration.essentials: The branch checks the counter and loops back if it isn't zero.
narration.detailed: `bnez t0, loop` compares the counter against zero. While it is non-zero the branch is taken, so the PC jumps back to the top of the loop and the body runs again.
narration.expert: The ALU evaluates the branch condition (t0 != 0 -> 1 = taken); the branch unit selects PC + immediate over PC + 4.

narration.essentials: After ten passes the total in a0 is 55.
narration.detailed: The counter has reached zero, so the branch falls through and the loop ends. a0 now holds 55 — the sum 10 + 9 + ... + 1.
